incorrects.description: Student selected 4 — counted only full symbols, missed that 9 is odd and needs a half-symbol

steps.text: 4 symbols would show 8 stickers. But Emma has 9—that's 1 more than 8. What can we use to show just 1 sticker?

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 — counted symbols as if each represents 1 sticker instead of 2

steps.text: Each symbol stands for 2 stickers, not 1. So 5 symbols would show 10 stickers total. Emma has 9—how many symbols for that?

incorrects.description: Student selected 9 — used the data value directly instead of calculating symbols needed

steps.text: 9 is how many stickers Emma has. But each symbol stands for 2 stickers. How many symbols do we need to show 9?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: System demonstrates calculating symbols for 9: 4 full symbols show 8, plus half-symbol shows 1 more

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Emma has 9 stickers. Each symbol stands for 2, so 4 full symbols show 8 stickers. We need 1 more sticker—that's a half-symbol. So 4 and a half symbols total.
